name: requests-html
documentation:
# Requests-HTML: HTML Parsing for Humans™ ```{image} https://farm5.staticflickr.com/4695/39152770914_a3ab8af40d_k_d.jpg ``` ```{image} https://travis-ci.org/kennethreitz/requests-html.svg?branch=master :target: https://travis-ci.org/kennethreitz/requests-html ``` This library intends to make parsing HTML (e.g. scraping the web) as simple and intuitive as possible. If you're interested in financially supporting Kenneth Reitz open source, consider [visiting this link](https://cash.me/$KennethReitz). Your support helps tremendously with sustainability of motivation, as Open Source is no longer part of my day job. When using this library you automatically get: - **Full JavaScript support**! - *CSS Selectors* (a.k.a jQuery-style, thanks to PyQuery). - *XPath Selectors*, for the faint at heart. - Mocked user-agent (like a real web browser). - Automatic following of redirects. - Connection–pooling and cookie persistence. - The Requests experience you know and love, with magical parsing abilities. - **Async Support** % Other nice features include: % % - Markdown export of pages and elements. # Tutorial & Usage Make a GET request to 'python.org', using Requests: ```pycon >>> from requests_html import HTMLSession >>> session = HTMLSession() >>> r = session.get('https://python.org/') ``` Try async and get some sites at the same time: ```pycon >>> from requests_html import AsyncHTMLSession >>> asession = AsyncHTMLSession() >>> async def get_pythonorg(): ... r = await asession.get('https://python.org/') >>> async def get_reddit(): ... r = await asession.get('https://reddit.com/') >>> async def get_google(): ... r = await asession.get('https://google.com/') >>> result = session.run(get_pythonorg, get_reddit, get_google) ``` Grab a list of all links on the page, as–is (anchors excluded): ```pycon >>> r.html.links {'//docs.python.org/3/tutorial/', '/about/apps/', 'https://github.com/python/pythondotorg/issues', '/accounts/login/', '/dev/peps/', '/about/legal/', 'https://www.python.org/accounts/login/', 'https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-364/', 'https://www.python.org/dev/core-mentorship/', 'https://www.python.org/about/success/#business', 'https://www.python.org/community/sigs/', 'https://www.python.org/events/python-user-group/', 'http://ipython.org', 'https://www.python.org/shell/', 'https://www.python.org/community/irc/', 'https://www.python.org/about/success/#engineering', 'http://www.pylonsproject.org/', 'http://pycon.blogspot.com/', 'https://www.python.org/about/success/#scientific', 'https://www.python.org/doc/essays/', 'http://www.djangoproject.com/', 'https://www.python.org/success-stories/industrial-light-magic-runs-python/', 'http://feedproxy.google.com/~r/PythonInsider/~3/x_c9D0S-4C4/python-370b1-is-now-available-for.html', 'http://wiki.python.org/moin/TkInter', 'https://www.python.org/jobs/', 'https://www.python.org/events/python-events/604/'} ``` Select an element with a CSS Selector: ```pycon >>> about = r.html.find('#about', first=True) ``` Grab an element's text contents: ```pycon >>> print(about.text) About Applications Quotes Getting Started Help Python Brochure ``` Introspect an Element's attributes: ```pycon >>> about.attrs {'id': 'about', 'class': ('tier-1', 'element-1'), 'aria-haspopup': 'true'} ``` Render out an Element's HTML: ```pycon >>> about.html '
  • \nAbout\n
      \n
    • Applications
      • \n
    • Quotes
      • \n
    • Getting Started
      • \n
    • Help
      • \n
    • Python Brochure
      • \n
    \n
    • ' ``` Select Elements within Elements: ```pycon >>> about.find('a') [, , , , , ] ``` Search for links within an element: ```pycon >>> about.absolute_links {'http://brochure.getpython.info/', 'https://www.python.org/about/gettingstarted/', 'https://www.python.org/about/', 'https://www.python.org/about/quotes/', 'https://www.python.org/about/help/', 'https://www.python.org/about/apps/'} ``` Search for text on the page: ```pycon >>> r.html.search('Python is a {} language')[0] programming ``` More complex CSS Selector example (copied from Chrome dev tools): ```pycon >>> r = session.get('https://github.com/') >>> sel = 'body > div.application-main > div.jumbotron.jumbotron-codelines > div > div > div.col-md-7.text-center.text-md-left > p' >>> print(r.html.find(sel, first=True).text) GitHub is a development platform inspired by the way you work. From open source to business, you can host and review code, manage projects, and build software alongside millions of other developers. ``` XPath is also supported: ```pycon >>> r.html.xpath('/html/body/div[1]/a') [] ``` # JavaScript Support Let's grab some text that's rendered by JavaScript: ```pycon >>> r = session.get('http://python-requests.org') >>> r.html.render() >>> r.html.search('Python 2 will retire in only {months} months!')['months'] '25' ``` Or you can do this async also: ```pycon >>> r = asession.get('http://python-requests.org/') >>> await r.html.arender() >>> r.html.search('Python 2 will retire in only {months} months!')['months'] '25' ``` Note, the first time you ever run the `render()` method, it will download Chromium into your home directory (e.g. `~/.pyppeteer/`). This only happens once. # Using without Requests You can also use this library without Requests: ```pycon >>> from requests_html import HTML >>> doc = """""" >>> html = HTML(html=doc) >>> html.links {'https://httpbin.org'} ``` # Installation ```shell $ pipenv install requests-html ✨🍰✨ ``` Only **Python 3.6** is supported.

functions.name: requests_html.user_agent
documentation:
Returns an apparently legit user-agent, if not requested one of a specific
style. Defaults to a Chrome-style User-Agent.

classes.name: requests_html.BaseParser

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.raw_html
documentation:
Bytes representation of the HTML content.
(`learn more <http://www.diveintopython3.net/strings.html>`_).

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.html
documentation:
Unicode representation of the HTML content
(`learn more <http://www.diveintopython3.net/strings.html>`_).

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.raw_html
documentation: Property setter for self.html.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.encoding
documentation:
The encoding string to be used, extracted from the HTML and
{class}`HTMLResponse <HTMLResponse>` headers.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.encoding
documentation: Property setter for self.encoding.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.pq
documentation:
[PyQuery](https://pythonhosted.org/pyquery/) representation
of the {class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.lxml
documentation:
[lxml](http://lxml.de) representation of the
{class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.text
documentation:
The text content of the
{class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.full_text
documentation:
The full text content (including links) of the
{class}`Element <Element>` or {class}`HTML <HTML>`.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.find
documentation:
Given a CSS Selector, returns a list of
{class}`Element <Element>` objects or a single one.

```
param selector

CSS Selector to use.

param clean

Whether or not to sanitize the found HTML of <script> and <style> tags.

param containing

If specified, only return elements that contain the provided text.

param first

Whether or not to return just the first result.

param _encoding

The encoding format.
```

Example CSS Selectors:

- `a`
- `a.someClass`
- `a#someID`
- `a[target=_blank]`

See W3School's [CSS Selectors Reference](https://www.w3schools.com/cssref/css_selectors.asp)
for more details.

If `first` is `True`, only returns the first
{class}`Element <Element>` found.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.xpath
documentation:
Given an XPath selector, returns a list of
{class}`Element <Element>` objects or a single one.

```
param selector

XPath Selector to use.

param clean

Whether or not to sanitize the found HTML of <script> and <style> tags.

param first

Whether or not to return just the first result.

param _encoding

The encoding format.
```

If a sub-selector is specified (e.g. `//a/@href`), a simple
list of results is returned.

See W3School's [XPath Examples](https://www.w3schools.com/xml/xpath_examples.asp)
for more details.

If `first` is `True`, only returns the first
{class}`Element <Element>` found.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.search
documentation:
Search the {class}`Element <Element>` for the given Parse template.

```
param template

The Parse template to use.
```

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.search_all
documentation:
Search the {class}`Element <Element>` (multiple times) for the given parse
template.

```
param template

The Parse template to use.
```

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.links
documentation: All found links on page, in as–is form.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser._make_absolute
documentation: Makes a given link absolute.

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.absolute_links
documentation:
All found links on page, in absolute form
(`learn more <https://www.navegabem.com/absolute-or-relative-links.html>`_).

methods.name: requests_html.BaseParser.base_url
documentation:
The base URL for the page. Supports the `<base>` tag
([learn more](https://www.w3schools.com/tags/tag_base.asp)).

A basic HTML/Element Parser, for Humans.

```
param element

The element from which to base the parsing upon.

param default_encoding

Which encoding to default to.

param html

HTML from which to base the parsing upon (optional).

param url

The URL from which the HTML originated, used for absolute_links.
```

classes.name: requests_html.Element

methods.name: requests_html.Element.attrs
documentation:
Returns a dictionary of the attributes of the {class}`Element <Element>`
([learn more](https://www.w3schools.com/tags/ref_attributes.asp)).

An element of HTML.

```
param element

The element from which to base the parsing upon.

param url

The URL from which the HTML originated, used for absolute_links.

param default_encoding

Which encoding to default to.
```

classes.name: requests_html.HTML

methods.name: requests_html.HTML.next
documentation:
Attempts to find the next page, if there is one. If `fetch`
is `True` (default), returns {class}`HTML <HTML>` object of
next page. If `fetch` is `False`, simply returns the next URL.

methods.name: requests_html.HTML._async_render
documentation: Handle page creation and js rendering. Internal use for render/arender methods.

methods.name: requests_html.HTML.render
documentation:
Reloads the response in Chromium, and replaces HTML content
with an updated version, with JavaScript executed.

```
param retries

The number of times to retry loading the page in Chromium.

param script

JavaScript to execute upon page load (optional).

param wait

The number of seconds to wait before loading the page, preventing timeouts (optional).

param scrolldown

Integer, if provided, of how many times to page down.

param sleep

Integer, if provided, of how many long to sleep after initial render.

param reload

If False, content will not be loaded from the browser, but will be provided from memory.

param keep_page

If True will allow you to interact with the browser page through r.html.page.
```

If `scrolldown` is specified, the page will scrolldown the specified
number of times, after sleeping the specified amount of time
(e.g. `scrolldown=10, sleep=1`).

If just `sleep` is provided, the rendering will wait *n* seconds, before
returning.

If `script` is specified, it will execute the provided JavaScript at
runtime. Example:

```python
script = """
    () => {
        return {
            width: document.documentElement.clientWidth,
            height: document.documentElement.clientHeight,
            deviceScaleFactor: window.devicePixelRatio,
        }
    }
"""
```

Returns the return value of the executed `script`, if any is provided:

```python
>>> r.html.render(script=script)
{'width': 800, 'height': 600, 'deviceScaleFactor': 1}
```

Warning: the first time you run this method, it will download
Chromium into your home directory (`~/.pyppeteer`).

methods.name: requests_html.HTML.arender
documentation: Async version of render. Takes same parameters.

An HTML document, ready for parsing.

```
param url

The URL from which the HTML originated, used for absolute_links.

param html

HTML from which to base the parsing upon (optional).

param default_encoding

Which encoding to default to.
```

classes.name: requests_html.HTMLResponse
documentation:
An HTML-enabled {class}`requests.Response <requests.Response>` object.
Effectively the same, but with an intelligent `.html` property added.

classes.name: requests_html.BaseSession

methods.name: requests_html.BaseSession.response_hook
documentation: Change response enconding and replace it by a HTMLResponse.

A consumable session, for cookie persistence and connection pooling,
amongst other things.

methods.name: requests_html.HTMLSession.close
documentation: If a browser was created close it first.

classes.name: requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession

methods.name: requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.__init__
documentation:
Set or create an event loop and a thread pool.

```
param loop

Asyncio loop to use.

param workers

Amount of threads to use for executing async calls.
If not pass it will default to the number of processors on the
machine, multiplied by 5.
```

methods.name: requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.request
documentation: Partial original request func and run it in a thread.

methods.name: requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.close
documentation: If a browser was created close it first.

methods.name: requests_html.AsyncHTMLSession.run
documentation:
Pass in all the coroutines you want to run, it will wrap each one
in a task, run it and wait for the result. Return a list with all
results, this is returned in the same order coros are passed in.
An async consumable session.
